    Global Search Dialog
      1. About RWJF
      2. How We Work
      3. Learning And Evaluation
      4. Ideas for an Equitable Future

      Ideas for An Equitable Future

        The equitable future we all deserve won’t simply arrive—we must create it together.

      We work with scientists, anthropologists, engineers, artists, and other creative thinkers across the nation to explore and accelerate cutting-edge ideas that could pave the way toward a future where health is no longer a privilege, but a right. Together, we can build a future where health is for everyone. 

      Funding Opportunity Closed

      Exploring Equitable Futures

      Our Exploring Equitable Futures call for proposals is now closed and we do not plan to open it again in 2026. Stay updated on funding opportunities at RWJF with our funding alerts.

      Sign up for Funding Alerts
      The future isn't fixed. Who gets to imagine it matters. featured item
      Featured item

      Report

      The future isn't fixed. Who gets to imagine it matters.

      Discover what happens when communities have the time, space, and resources to imagine futures that uplift everyone.

      Ideas for an Equitable Future

      Related Initiatives

      New technologies, scientific discoveries and cultural shifts are redefining our future. We support research and pilot projects that shine a light on how these forces are shaping our future and uncover solutions that can help us tap into these emerging trends. The initiatives featured here are examples of ideas that could help us achieve health equity faster and together.

      Challenge Assumptions

      Rather than leading to bad decisions, "crowdsourcing" can actually improve medical decisionmaking.

      Visit Network Dynamics Group

      Upend the Status Quo

      Gig work can be made more equitable with fair pay, stable hours, and safe working conditions.

      Visit aspeninstitute.org

      Explore Emerging Trends

      Could generative AI promote collaboration, compassion, and other behaviors in online communities?

      Visit news.yale.edu

      Expand Thinking

      Often overlooked as a health issue, noise can trigger stress and heart-related conditions.

      Visit communitynoiselab.org

      Offer New Perspectives

      Nurses with a "maker" mindset are improving care at the bedside. 

      Visit makernurse.com

      Ideas from Other Fields

      Using Afrofuturism, high school kids are imagining a healthier future where Black and Brown people thrive.

      Visit mocha.org

      Want more futuring in your inbox?

      Sign up for the latest news and updates on futures thinking as we explore the ways we can expand our collective perceptions of what’s possible. Feel free to reach out to us directly at .

      Sign up

      Related Content

       

      Research

      Work and Health: What Does the Future Hold?

      RWJF is exploring how changes in the nature and structure of work may impact the health equity and well-being of people, their families, and their communities.
      Social Determinants of Health
      Blog Post

      How the Future of Work May Impact Our Wellbeing

      The health of workers in a rapidly changing work environment is often overlooked. In a time when incomes, schedules, and healthcare are becoming less predictable.
      Healthcare Coverage and Access Ideas for an Equitable Future
      Blog Post

      How the Maker Movement is Innovating Healthcare

      Nurses are natural problem solvers. That's why MakerNurse encourages nurses to continue devising their own solutions, then give them the tools to create them.
      Healthcare Quality and Value Ideas for an Equitable Future
      Research

      Evidence and Health: What Does the Future Hold?

      As we build the evidence base leading to wellbeing and health equity, we must harness the research trends and ideas that are most promising, while being vigilant in mitigating potential harms that could arise from these shifts.
      Public and Community Health

      Featured Resources

       

      Candid Conversations about Who Gets to Imagine the Future

      Six visionary thinkers pair up to talk about the power of “futuring”—anticipating, imagining, and designing possible tomorrows.

      Listen to episodes

      Candid Conversations about the Future of Research

      In this five-part series, six visionary researchers challenged us to rethink the way society produces, collects, shares, and uses knowledge about health. 

      Listen to episodes

      Hunches

      A hunch is an inkling, a possibility, a sneaking suspicion about the future. We don’t often share our hunches, but when we do, amazing things can happen. Share yours!

      Visit shareyourhunch.org

      What’s Next Health

      Our publication explores a range of signals, emerging trends, and pioneering ideas that can help us all anticipate and influence a more equitable future.

      Visit medium.com

      Take Us to a Better Place: Stories

      Using the power of fiction, this collection of 10 short stories published by RWJF helps readers imagine possible pathways to a future where health is for everyone.

      Ideas for an Equitable Future
      Read the stories

      Stay informed

      Sign up for the latest funding alerts, news, research, and perspectives from RWJF.

      Email address already subscribed

      Email address already subscribed. Please check your inbox to manage your subscriptions.

      Subscribed!

      Thank you. You are now subscribed.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.

      Which profession or pursuit best describes you?
      Area(s) Of Interest
